summary: Work_1 implementation satisfies the execution contract requirements with a single enforced execution model, deterministic pipeline runner, explicit stage invocation, and validated fail-fast behavior. Provided diff and code evidence are sufficient to verify correctness.
reasons: invoke-stage.js defines a single execution model (spawn-sync-explicit) using spawnSync with no alternative execution paths, satisfying 'single execution model' requirement.
run-pipeline.js executes stages strictly sequentially and enforces fail-fast behavior by immediately returning on first failed stage, fulfilling acceptance criteria.
default-stages.js defines concrete stage implementations (ingest_bootstrap, ingest_verify_ref) using invokeStage, ensuring execution contract is exercised and not abstract.
Explicit input passing is implemented via environment variables (PIPELINE_VIDEO_PATH, PIPELINE_OUTPUT_DIR, PIPELINE_INPUT_VIDEO_REF), satisfying 'no implicit behavior' requirement.
pipeline-context.js ensures deterministic and explicit path resolution for all workspace and artifact paths under output_dir.
CLI entrypoint (src/cli.js) enforces required arguments (--video, --output-dir), validates input readability, and propagates inputs into runPipeline.
Tests (run-pipeline.test.js, cli.test.js, pipeline-context.test.js) verify deterministic execution order, fail-fast behavior, execution model usage, and path resolution.
No artifact mutation or processing logic is introduced in the runner beyond orchestration responsibilities, maintaining architectural constraints.
Provided git diff and untracked file contents include full implementation and tests under allowed_paths, satisfying review evidence requirements.
